channel.title: Context Engineering Daily - Research Papers
channel.description: Latest Research Papers news in Context Engineering

item.title: Computational conceptual history of scientific concepts: From early digital methods to LLMs
item.description: arXiv:2606.04118v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: This article situates large language models (LLMs) within the longer history of computational approaches to concept analysis in the history, philosophy, and sociology of science (HPSS). We examine what LLMs add to existing methods, how they inherit lo...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> LLM, analysis, research, arxiv, large language model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: A Systematic Analysis of Linguistic Features in AI-Generated Text Detection Across Domains and Models
item.description: arXiv:2606.04177v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Interpretable linguistic features offer a promising approach for explaining why a given text appears machine-generated, particularly for non-expert users. However, existing findings on which features reliably indicate LLM-generated text remain fragmen...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> LLM, analysis, arxiv, RAG, context | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Cross-Prompt Generalization in Detecting AI-Generated Fake News Using Interpretable Linguistic Features
item.description: arXiv:2606.04199v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The increasing use of large language models has raised concerns about the spread of AI-generated fake news, particularly under varying prompting strategies. Most existing detection models are trained and evaluated under a single generation setting, le...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> prompting, analysis, framework, arxiv, large language model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: SMAC-Talk: A Natural Language Extension of the StarCraft Multi-Agent Challenge for Large Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2606.04202v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: As LLMs become more widely deployed, they are increasingly expected to work alongside other AI agents rather than operating in isolation. Effective coordination in these settings requires agents to communicate, share information and make decisions und...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> LLM, study, research, release, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: VAMPS: Visual-Assisted Mathematical Problem Solving Benchmark
item.description: arXiv:2606.04244v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Multimodal large language models are increasingly capable of complex reasoning, yet their performance often degrades when they must externalize a problem through a tool and then reason over the tool's output, specifically when they rely on visual aids...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> LLM, tool, analysis, multimodal, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Can Generalist Agents Automate Data Curation?
item.description: arXiv:2606.04261v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Curating training data is among the most consequential yet labor-intensive parts of modern AI development: practitioners iteratively propose, implement, evaluate, and revise data policies against noisy benchmark feedback. We ask whether generalist cod...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> instruction, paper, prompting, analysis, vision | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: The Saturation Trap and the Subjectivity of Intervention Timing: Why Affect-Based Triggers and LLM Judges Fail to Time Interventions on Autonomous Agents
item.description: arXiv:2606.04296v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: As autonomous AI agents move from conversational systems to long-horizon software execution, runtime safety layers that decide when to interrupt an agent have become essential. We study this timing problem using a continuous 18-dimensional affective-d...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> GPT, LLM, arxiv, zero-shot, context | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Do Transformers Need Three Projections? Systematic Study of QKV Variants
item.description: arXiv:2606.04032v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Transformers have become the standard solution for various AI tasks, with the query, key, and value (QKV) attention formulation playing a central role. However, the individual contribution of these three projections and the impact of omitting some rem...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> transformer, study, experiment, vision, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Inverse Critical Experiment Design via Gradient Optimization and a Multigroup Attention-Based Neural Network Architecture
item.description: arXiv:2606.04033v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The validation of advanced nuclear reactor designs and fuel concepts requires critical experiments with high neutronic similarity to the target technology. Neutronic similarity is quantified by the correlation coefficient $c_k$, which captures the sha...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> experiment, arxiv, RAG, vector, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Unlocking Feature Learning in Gated Delta Networks at Scale
item.description: arXiv:2606.04048v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Training and scaling Large Language Models demand enormous computational resources, motivating both efficient sub-quadratic architectures and principled hyperparameter tuning methods. While the Maximal Update Parametrization ($\mu$P) has enabled zero-...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> transformer, analysis, experiment, arxiv, zero-shot | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: ACAT: A Collaborative Platform for Efficient Aspect-Based Sentiment Dataset Annotation
item.description: arXiv:2606.04189v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Aspect-Based Sentiment Analysis (ABSA) requires high-quality datasets to train reliable models. However, existing annotation tools treat output as flat files, leaving researchers to manually consolidate multi-annotator data, reconstruct relational str...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> tool, paper, analysis, research, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Position: Deployed Reinforcement Learning should be Continual
item.description: arXiv:2606.04029v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Reinforcement Learning (RL) has received increasing attention and adoption in real-world use cases. Most of these systems follow a train-then-fix paradigm, where trained agents do not learn while interacting with the world until performance degrades a...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> example, paper, attention, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Stumbling Into AI Emotional Dependence: How Routine AI Interactions Reshape Human Connection
item.description: arXiv:2606.04150v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Public discourse and emerging policy typically assume that AI emotional support is a deliberate act: a lonely user consciously seeking comfort from a dedicated companion chatbot. In this paper, we draw on emerging empirical evidence and argue that thi...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> paper, study, arxiv, platform | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Characterizing initial human-AI proof formalization workflows
item.description: arXiv:2606.04273v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: For centuries, human mathematicians have written proofs to substantiate their mathematical arguments; yet, the ability to automatically verify the validity of proofs has long been a challenge. Advances in AI systems' ability to generate code and engag...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> tool, analysis, vision, arxiv, study | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Early Detection of Alzheimer's Disease Using Explainable Machine Learning on Clinical Biomarkers: A Multi-Class Classification Study Using the Alzheimer's Disease Neuroimaging Initiative (ADNI) Dataset
item.description: arXiv:2606.03995v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Background: Alzheimer's disease (AD) affects over 55 million people worldwide. Accurate, interpretable detection of normal cognition (NC), mild cognitive impairment (MCI), and AD from routine clinical assessments remains a critical unmet need. Methods...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> analysis, multimodal, framework, arxiv, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Pseudospectral Bounds for Transient Amplification in Coupled Gradient Descent
item.description: arXiv:2606.04031v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Coupled gradient descent--where the update of one parameter block depends on another--underlies bilevel optimization, two-time-scale stochastic approximation, and adversarial training. When the coupled Jacobian is block-triangular, asymptotic stabilit...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> experiment, framework, arxiv, analysis | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Bayes-Sufficient Representations in Supervised Learning
item.description: arXiv:2606.04045v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Representation learning is often described as preserving the information in an input that is relevant for prediction. This work asks what relevance means for a fixed supervised decision problem. A representation is defined to be Bayes-sufficient for a...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> framework, arxiv, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)
